message:
fix(ft-api): functional_normalize composes through autograd primitives [bd-c5g4]

functional_normalize used to extract values, divide in plain f64,
and rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing
autograd through every L2-normalization layer (used in retrieval /
contrastive / metric-learning models). PyTorch's
torch.nn.functional.normalize IS differentiable.

Compose through autograd-aware primitives:
   abs_x       = tensor_abs(input)
   pow_x       = tensor_pow(abs_x, p)
   sum_x       = tensor_sum_dim(pow_x, dim)
   norm        = tensor_pow(sum_x, 1.0 / p)
   norm_c      = tensor_clamp_min(norm, 1e-12)
   broadcast   = shape with dim set to 1
   norm_reshaped = tensor_reshape(norm_c, broadcast)
   norm_b      = tensor_expand(norm_reshaped, shape)
   y           = tensor_div(input, norm_b)

Reshaping to an explicit keepdim=true-style broadcast shape
(rather than tensor_unsqueeze) makes the rank handling uniform
across 1-D and higher-rank inputs — tensor_sum_dim returns rank
N-1 for higher-rank inputs but rank 1 (= [1]) for 1-D inputs, so
unsqueeze would mis-rank in the 1-D case.

Adds functional_normalize_propagates_gradient_through_input
regression test:
  input = [3, 4], p=2, dim=0
  y = [3/5, 4/5] = [0.6, 0.8]
  Under sum loss (grad_y = [1, 1]):
    grad_x_i = (1 - y_i * sum_y) / ||x||_2
    grad = [(1 - 0.6 * 1.4) / 5, (1 - 0.8 * 1.4) / 5]
         = [0.032, -0.024]
asserts to 1e-12.

Closes frankentorch-c5g4. ft-api lib tests: 1156 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -69,6 +69,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-c36b","title":"Linear algebra ops lack subprocess conformance: svd, qr, cholesky, det, slogdet, inv, pinverse, solve, eigh, lstsq, matrix_norm","description":"FEATURE_PARITY.md claims parity_green for these ops but none have subprocess conformance against numpy.linalg / scipy.linalg. Add diff-tests across well-conditioned and edge-case matrices (identity, scaled identity, singular, near-singular, complex eigenstructure). Pattern: matches torch_vector_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance / torch_diff_numpy_subprocess_conformance — numpy oracle, ULP- or relative-tolerant comparison. Coverage: at least det / slogdet / inv / solve as the highest-impact subset; the others can follow incrementally.","notes":"All available linalg slices added across recent commits: det (earlier round), slogdet (e8cfc36), inv (75dbb04), solve (7115a2a), cholesky (ea904bb), qr (9a86171), svd (62ac52d), pinv (6e86432), eigh (a4b2758), lstsq (this commit). matrix_norm has no separate API in ft-api (vector_norm covers the existing surface and was already locked by torch_vector_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance). Ten subprocess harnesses against numpy.linalg, all fail-loud, with structural-invariant comparisons for the sign-ambiguous decompositions (qr/svd/eigh) and element-wise comparisons for the unique outputs (det/slogdet/inv/solve/cholesky/pinv/lstsq). One real bug surfaced: SVD doesn't complete the orthonormal basis on rank-deficient inputs — tracked under frankentorch-zs8a.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:20:37.714643912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:11:05.951874677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T22:11:05.949322742Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c3d","title":"Add CUDA/GPU execution backend","description":"FrankenTorch currently exposes `Device::Cuda` tags and device-guard validation, but all real execution remains CPU-only. There are no CUDA kernels, no device memory model, no dispatcher backend selection beyond CPU, and no runtime integration for GPU execution.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Add concrete GPU storage/device abstractions beyond enum tagging
2. Implement CUDA dispatch keys and backend routing
3. Port core kernels (elementwise, reductions, matmul, indexing) to GPU
4. Preserve deterministic autograd/DAC evidence across cross-device execution
5. Add conformance and benchmark coverage for CPU↔GPU parity

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can truthfully mark CUDA/GPU as deferred rather than silently omitted.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:23:50.059185557Z","created_by":"CopperStork","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:33:49.271814166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c4tx","title":"masked_scatter all-false fast path drops source zero-gradient","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs. The current tensor_masked_scatter implementation returns Ok(input) immediately when n_true == 0 at lines 11682-11688. That preserves input passthrough but removes the source tensor from the backward graph entirely. The adjacent regression test masked_scatter_all_false_mask_passes_input_through_with_grad at lines 33742-33761 says source gradient should be zero, but it only asserts input_grad, so the missing source gradient is not covered. Repro by code inspection: create input requires_grad=true, all-zero mask, source requires_grad=true, call tensor_masked_scatter, sum the output, run tensor_backward, then query tensor_gradient(report, source). Because the fast path returns the original input node, compute_reachable in ft-autograd only reaches input and tensor_gradient(source) is None instead of a zero vector of source.numel(). This silently violates the deterministic autograd contract for a tracked source argument. Fix by composing the all-false case through an autograd-aware zero dependency on source, or by adding an explicit masked_scatter op/backward rule that records source and emits zero source gradients when no mask entries consume it. Add a regression assertion that all-false masks produce source_grad == vec![0.0; source_numel].","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:00:50.510404660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:34:57.725416487Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T18:34:57.723645605Z","close_reason":"Removed the n_true==0 fast-path return-input shortcut for non-empty source — the gather + where composition now runs unconditionally for source_numel >= 1, so source stays in the backward graph and tensor_gradient(source) returns zeros(source.shape) instead of None. Empty-source case kept as a fast path since its gradient is shape-empty regardless. Added masked_scatter_all_false_mask_with_empty_source_returns_input edge-case test and strengthened masked_scatter_all_false_mask_passes_input_through_with_grad to assert source_grad == zeros.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-c5g4","title":"functional_normalize severs autograd; composable through abs/pow/sum_dim/div","description":"functional_normalize at lib.rs:5151 computes y = x / max(||x||_p along dim, eps) but the body extracts values, divides in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing autograd through every L2-normalization layer. PyTorch's torch.nn.functional.normalize IS differentiable.

Compose through autograd primitives:
   abs_x   = tensor_abs(input)
   pow_x   = tensor_pow(abs_x, p)
   sum_x   = tensor_sum_dim(pow_x, dim)
   norm    = tensor_pow(sum_x, 1.0 / p)
   norm_c  = tensor_clamp_min(norm, eps)
   norm_kd = tensor_unsqueeze(norm_c, dim)
   norm_b  = tensor_expand(norm_kd, original_shape)
   y       = tensor_div(input, norm_b)

All ops are autograd-aware so gradients flow back to input. Sister of frankentorch-7yvv (dropout severance) — both are critical for training paths.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed functional_normalize through abs/pow/sum_dim/clamp_min/reshape/expand/div primitives — same observable y = x / max(||x||_p along dim, eps) but with the autograd tape preserved.

The norm reduction is reshaped to a keepdim=true-style broadcast shape (input's shape with dim set to 1) so the subsequent expand + div work uniformly across 1-D and higher-rank inputs.

Added functional_normalize_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test for input=[3,4] L2-normalize on dim=0:
   y = [0.6, 0.8] under sum loss → grad = [(1 - y_i * sum_y) / ||x||_2] = [0.032, -0.024]
asserts to 1e-12.

ft-api lib tests: 1156.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:31:08.472775869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:34:38.374626121Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:34:38.371420108Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c7c","title":"Add end-to-end training example and integration test","description":"FrankenTorch has e2e training loop tests buried in ft-optim's test suite, but there is no standalone, documented example showing users how to train a model. This is critical for proving the system works end-to-end and for user onboarding.

Create a comprehensive integration test (in ft-conformance or a new examples/ directory) that:

1. Defines a 2-layer MLP: Linear(784, 128) -> ReLU -> Linear(128, 10)
2. Generates synthetic MNIST-like data using the existing TensorDataset/DataLoader
3. Trains for 5 epochs using Adam optimizer with learning rate scheduling (StepLR or CosineAnnealing)
4. Tracks loss per epoch and asserts it decreases monotonically (with tolerance for noise)
5. Uses no_grad context for evaluation pass
6. Saves model state_dict to file, loads it back, verifies predictions match
7. Uses BatchNorm or LayerNorm in the architecture to exercise normalization paths
8. Logs: epoch number, batch count, loss per epoch, final accuracy estimate, elapsed time

This test exercises the full stack: ft-data (DataLoader) -> ft-nn (modules) -> ft-api (forward/backward) -> ft-autograd (gradient computation) -> ft-optim (parameter update) -> ft-serialize (save/load).

If any component fails to compose correctly, this test catches it.

Tests:
- The example IS the test: it should be a #[test] fn in ft-conformance or similar
- Assert loss decreases over 5 epochs
- Assert save/load round-trip produces identical predictions
- Assert no_grad eval context doesn't corrupt gradient state
- Log detailed per-epoch metrics for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:01.270413750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:14:50.299054985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:14:50.298796636Z","close_reason":"Added 5 E2E integration tests exercising the full stack: MLP regression with DataLoader/Adam/StepLR, binary classification with cross-entropy, save/load round-trip verification, no_grad eval safety, dataloader epoch shuffle. All pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c84","title":"Diagnose residual beads integrity-check warning","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:27:54.222375196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:35:07.976091802Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:35:07.975908695Z","close_reason":"Diagnosed residual integrity warning: live DB and JSONL stay in sync; isolated rebuild reproduces the warning; VACUUM yields a clean DB; subsequent normal br write paths reintroduce 'never used' pages, pointing to a beads_rust/frankensqlite writer/page-accounting bug rather than JSONL drift.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-cg0","title":"Align detailed crate contract table with current FrankenTorch crate names","description":"Update section 14 of COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md so the crate contract table uses ft-core, ft-kernel-cpu, ft-api, ft-runtime, ft-device, and ft-data instead of the older ft-types/ft-tensor/ft-ops/frankentorch naming.","notes":"Updated section 14 of the comprehensive spec so the detailed crate contract table now uses the actual workspace crates: ft-core, ft-device, ft-kernel-cpu, ft-api, ft-data, ft-runtime, plus the existing ft-dispatch/ft-autograd/ft-nn/ft-optim/ft-serialize/ft-conformance entries.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:18:43.486151087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:19:12.519560308Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:19:12.518714757Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
